The fire being taken from between the wheels, under the cherubim, appeared to signify the wrath of God to be executed upon Jerusalem. It indicated that the fire of Divine wrath, which kindles judgment upon a people, is just and holy; and on the great day, the earth, and all the works in it, will be burnt up.

Ezekiel sees the working of Divine providence in the government of the lower world and its affairs. When God leaves a people in displeasure, angels above and all events below further his departure. The Spirit of life, the Spirit of God, directs all creatures, in heaven and on earth, so as to make them serve the Divine purpose. God removes from a provoking people by degrees; and when ready to depart, would return to them if they were a repenting, praying people. Let this warn sinners to seek the Lord while he may be found, and to call on him while he is near, and cause us all to walk humbly and watchfully with our God.
